Executive Summary · 2026-05-18

556 customer-risk signals across 189 accounts in the 30-day window. 43 accounts are in the Red Zone (hitting all three buckets — CNS, P2V, and PGF — simultaneously). Dominant risk theme is PGF at 55% of signals. Seven-day volume is up -2 vs the prior seven days (153 vs 155).

  • Ali Fraser (EMEA) carries the most exposure: 23 Red Zone accounts across 67 books (210 signals).
  • Rising vs prior 7d: Pizza Express (+6), Bamford Collection Limited (+6), Modern Restaurant Concepts (+4).
  • Cooling vs prior 7d: Flynn Restaurant Group LLC (-5), Splendid Restaurants (Colonel) Ltd (-4), Pizza Hut - Yum! III (UK) Limited (-4).

About GTM Risk Intelligence

This dashboard surfaces customer-risk signals lifted directly from Gong call recordings. Every alert on this page originated as a quote captured during a live customer call that matched one of three Gong AI trackers configured for Fourth's GTM team.

A single call can fire more than one tracker — a customer can be simultaneously under-supported, questioning value, and blocked by a missing feature. When that happens, we count each tracker hit independently. Accounts that fire all three trackers enter the Red Zone — those are the accounts leadership should look at first.

Below are the exact tracker definitions that drive categorisation. These are the instructions Gong is following when it decides whether an alert belongs in CNS, P2V, or PGF.

CNSCategory 1 — Customer Not Feeling Supported

Question: Is the customer not feeling supported?

Tracker Description (Gong)

Detects calls where the customer expresses frustration or loss of confidence related to the support experience — including slow response times, lack of a consistent point of contact, feeling deprioritized, or receiving reactive rather than proactive guidance. Also flags calls where the customer indicates they are not getting value from onboarding, training, or implementation support, and feel left to figure things out on their own.

PGFCategory 2 — Product Gaps & Issues Lacking Features

Question: Is the customer needing product or features developed?

Tracker Description (Gong)

Detects calls where the customer describes functionality that is missing, broken, or so rigid that it forces them to work around the system rather than through it. Covers all product areas: scheduling, payroll, forecasting, reporting, integrations, and absence management. Key signal is the customer describing manual effort that should be automated, a workaround using external tools (especially Excel or spreadsheets), a missing feature they expected to exist, or a system error or data inaccuracy they cannot resolve inside the platform.

P2VCategory 3 — Price-to-Value / Not Getting Money's Worth

Question: Is the customer not seeing enough value for the cost?

Tracker Description (Gong)

Detects calls where the customer signals that the cost of the platform is not justified by the value they are receiving. This includes direct complaints about pricing or contract terms, confusion or frustration around billing, unexpected fees, or a perception that they are paying for capabilities they cannot use or do not have access to. Also detects renewal risk language and competitive pricing comparisons.

How the risk score is calculated

risk_score = (bucket_count × 10) + total_mentions
  • bucket_count — distinct trackers (CNS / P2V / PGF) this account fired in the 30-day window. Ranges 1–3.
  • total_mentions — total alerts for this account across the window.
Design intent: accounts hitting all three buckets always rank above accounts with heavy volume in a single bucket. Mention count breaks ties within the same bucket-count tier.
🔴
Red Zone = accounts where bucket_count == 3. A customer hitting all three trackers in the same 30-day window is the strongest signal Gong can give us that something's wrong at the account. These are the accounts worth a proactive call from the GTM leader before next week.

Data source & refresh cadence

  • Source: #gong_gtm-test Slack channel. Gong posts one alert per tracker hit per call.
  • Window: rolling 30 days, refreshed every morning.
  • Dedup: each unique (call_id, bucket) pair counts as one signal — if Gong re-fires the same tracker on the same call, we don't double-count.
  • Mapping: accounts are joined to GTM leaders (K.G. / David Peirce / Ali Fraser) via knowledge/gtm-mapping-data.csv. Accounts whose rep isn't yet in the mapping show as Unassigned.
  • "Unknown" accounts: when the parser can't extract a company name from the Gong alert text, those signals pile into a single Unknown row. Treat any high score on Unknown as a parsing-quality flag, not a single account in crisis.

How to read this dashboard

  • Exec View — KPIs + executive summary + top 20 Red Zone with CNS/P2V/PGF cell counts and Δ vs prior 7 days. Click any account row to drill into full call detail.
  • Ops View — all accounts, searchable and filterable by bucket, leader, or Red Zone status. Same drill-down on click.
  • Rising / Cooling — 7-day-over-7-day deltas per account. This is the truest "what's changed" read and is immune to window-narrowing effects that can make the 30-day total wobble across runs.
